title: Update-time schema-version-advancing write paths never register a migration declaration
description: updateContext's merge branch (locally-modified/both-diverged resync), resolveConflict, and write-schema --operation replace can each advance an installed schema's declared version. None registers a migration declaration. Only resyncSchema's catalog-ahead migrate branch does this: it walks findCatalogMigrationChain and calls appendMigrationDeclForDir per hop. Distinct from FGAP-140 (install-time seeding gap) -- this is update-time, on an already-installed substrate.

evidence.reference: updateContext merge branch (conflicts-empty arm) calls writeSchemaCheckedForDir and stampBaselineFromBody, no appendMigrationDeclForDir call anywhere in the arm
resolveConflict calls writeSchemaCheckedForDir and stampBaselineFromBody only, no migration registration call in the function body

writeSchemaCheckedForDir's replace path meta-validates and writes the schema body only; no version-delta read, no migration registration; doc comment states migrating existing block items is explicitly out of scope for this op, but registering a decl that only enables a future read to resolve a chain is a distinct, unaddressed concern

resyncSchema's migrate branch is the sole correct precedent: findCatalogMigrationChain resolves the catalog's authored chain, appendMigrationDeclForDir registers each hop verbatim before any data validation runs

impact: A schema whose version advances through any of the three unaddressed paths becomes unreadable via the canonical read path once its block data's stamped schema_version predates the new version, with no self-heal path. Reproduced live on this project's own active substrate: research (schema at 1.4.0, block data stamped 1.2.0, chain registered only to 1.3.0) and session-notes (schema at 1.1.0, block data stamped 1.0.0, zero registered migrations) both throw MigrationRegistry errors on read.
proposed_resolution: Add registerCatalogMigrationChainIfKnown(destRoot, samplesRoot, schemaName, fromVersion, toVersion) adjacent to resyncSchema: calls findCatalogMigrationChain; on a complete chain, registers each hop via appendMigrationDeclForDir copying its actual authored kind/transform verbatim; on no chain, returns null with a reason and registers nothing -- never fabricates an identity decl, since research's own 1.0.1-to-1.1.0 hop is a real content transform, not identity. Call it from updateContext's merge branch, resolveConflict, and write-schema --operation replace's commit path, each right after the existing stampBaselineFromBody call.
